title: Calculate a Tip
summary: Quickly figure out a fair tip at a restaurant or for a service.
learnerPromise: You will be able to estimate a reasonable tip using mental math.
whyItMatters: Tipping is a common way to show appreciation for good service, and knowing how to calculate it quickly helps you pay fairly without stress.
realLifeUses: Leaving a tip after a meal at a sit-down restaurant
Tipping a hairdresser, delivery driver, or taxi driver
Splitting a bill and adding tip as a group
Checking that an automatic gratuity on a large bill is reasonable
result: You can calculate 10%, 15%, and 20% of a bill in under a minute.
youWillLearn: Move the decimal to find 10% of any amount
Find the halfway point between 10% and 20% for 15%
Round to the nearest dollar for simplicity
miniChallenge: Pick a restaurant bill total and calculate 15% and 20% tip amounts.
steps: Look at the pre-tax bill total (the subtotal for food and drinks).
Move the decimal point one place to the left to find 10%.
Decide whether service was standard (15%) or excellent (20%).
For 15%, take half of the 10% amount and add it to the 10%. For 20%, double the 10%.
Round to the nearest whole dollar if you prefer simple numbers.
completionCriteria: You calculated a 15% and 20% tip for the same bill total
You can explain the trick to a friend in one sentence
commonProblems: Using the post-tax total instead of the pre-tax subtotal
Forgetting to check if gratuity was already added
Feeling unsure how much to tip for takeout or counter service
tips: It is okay to round; exact pennies are not required
Some people tip on the post-tax total for simplicity; either way is generally accepted
If the service was poor, it is reasonable to tip less or speak with a manager
